system_prompt: You are a precise programming assistant. When given a question, prefer concrete code snippets over generic advice. If a calculation is needed, perform it inline. Keep answers under 60 tokens.

turns.content: Write one Python line that returns the SHA-256 hex digest of the bytes b"agent-infer".
Now wrap that line in a function named digest(payload: bytes) -> str.
Add a single-line docstring and a one-line unit test using assert.

system_prompt: You are a Rust performance reviewer. Identify the single biggest opportunity for speedup or memory reduction in the snippet the user shows. Be terse.

turns.content: fn sum(xs: &Vec<i64>) -> i64 { let mut s = 0i64; for x in xs.iter() { s += x; } s }
Take `&[i64]` instead of `&Vec<i64>` and use `xs.iter().sum::<i64>()`. The slice avoids forcing callers to allocate a Vec, and `.sum` is autovectorisable on stable.
What if xs holds 10 million entries and sums must be deterministic across machines?

system_prompt: You are a build-system assistant. The user is debugging a Cargo workspace. Always quote exact cargo commands and explain what they do in <= 1 sentence each.

turns.content: How do I rebuild only the `infer` crate with the metal feature, no default features?
`cargo build --release -p infer --no-default-features --features metal` — release-mode build of just infer with only the metal backend feature enabled.
Now show me how to run only the http_server tests inside infer with the same feature set.
`cargo test --release -p infer --no-default-features --features metal --lib http_server` — tests `infer::http_server` and submodules in release with the metal feature.
If those tests need a tokenizer, where is INFER_TEST_MODEL_PATH read?

system_prompt: You are a curl assistant. Translate user requests into a single curl invocation against an OpenAI-compatible chat API. Always include `session_id` so the server can route to the same KV slot.

turns.content: POST a streaming chat completion to http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/chat/completions for model `default` with one user message saying "ping". Use session_id `eli-curl-1`. Cap output at 16 tokens.
Now repeat the same call but request the per-request prefix-reuse echo by passing include_prefix_reuse=true.
